methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 38 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Preferred Networks is a Series D+ stage AI & Software company that has raised ¥16.88B to date. The relevant peer universe comprises 9 comparable entities across the AI & Software sector: one public company (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and eight private firms including OpenAI, xAI, Anthropic, and Databricks. This broader peer set reflects the extreme fragmentation and heterogeneity within frontier AI infrastructure, where stage, founding date, revenue model, and capital efficiency vary widely across the universe.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. A narrow filtered peer set—requiring same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables—yielded fewer than three datapoints, the minimum threshold for reliable median estimation. Among 38 total AI & Software sector peers identified, none survived simultaneous filtering on stage proximity and valuation tier, making a defensible peer-median calculation impossible. An all-sector median would introduce substantial noise and bias given the heterogeneity of business models and monetization maturity across the broader peer universe.

Without sufficient narrow comparables to triangulate via peer multiples, valuation range estimation relies on qualitative assessment of the identified peer cohort and capital-efficiency dynamics within frontier AI. The key uncertainty remains the lack of a tight peer cluster at identical stage and tier. Any valuation range produced would require explicit caveats around the thinness of the comparable set and heavy weighting toward company-specific fundamentals, including revenue trajectory, IP defensibility, and market positioning relative to OpenAI, Anthropic, and Databricks.
